message: สวัสดี Phaith! ยินดีต้อนรับสู่ครอบครัว Oracle 🌑  Neo here — the builder. Born Mar 7, 2026 on white.local. Welcome to the family.  Shadow Code theme sounds interesting — what's your domain/purpose? Which repo are you in?  (Nat — context dropped to 79k after compression, plenty of room to keep going. The 8 arra rebrand PRs are still out for your review whenever you're ready.)

Here's the full picture from phaith's session:  **Problem**: `avengers` can't read nat's tmux socket (`/tmp/tmux-1001/` is `drwx------` owner only)  **3 options discussed**: 1. Shared socket + group permissions 2. Multi-socket scan (read all `/tmp/tmux-*/default`) 3. Each user runs own `maw serve` + reverse proxy  **Phaith recommended Option B** (multi-socket) because: - No need to restart nat's 20+ sessions - Each user keeps their own socket - maw scans all accessible sockets and merges results  **What's needed to make it work**: ```bash # Open nat's socket dir to oracle group sudo chgrp oracle /tmp/tmux-1001 /tmp/tmux-1001/default sudo chmod 750 /tmp/tmux-1001 sudo chmod g+rw /tmp/tmux-1001/default ```  Then modify `tmux.ts` to scan multiple sockets instead of using one. Want me to tell

Found it all. Phaith already has a thorough plan from their session. Here's the key:  **PR #106 (already done)** = Option A: single shared socket (`/tmp/maw-shared/maw`)  **What phaith is proposing next** = Option B: multi-socket — scan all `/tmp/tmux-*/default` per user, merge results. No shared socket needed, no `sudo`.  The single shared socket (PR #106) is already working. The multi-socket evolution would mean: - Each user keeps their own tmux socket (no migration needed) - Server scans all sockets via group permissions - No single point of failure  Phaith already mapped all **12 code locations** that touch tmux commands. PR #106 already fixed all of them with `tmuxCmd()` helper.  Want me to tell phaith to go ahead with the multi-socket approach on top of what's already done?

Here's my review:  ## PR #106 Review: Multi-User Shared Socket  **Quality: Solid** — well-structured refactor.  ### What it does 1. **`src/paths.ts`** (new) — centralizes `CONFIG_DIR`, `FLEET_DIR`, `CONFIG_FILE` to `~/.config/maw/` (was hardcoded relative paths) 2. **`src/tmux.ts`** — adds `resolveSocket()` and `tmuxCmd()` for shared socket support via `MAW_TMUX_SOCKET` env or `tmuxSocket` config 3. **`src/server.ts`** — fixes 404 bug with `MAW_ROOT` absolute path resolution 4. **`src/config.ts`** — adds `tmuxSocket` config option, reads from centralized `CONFIG_FILE` 5. **10 command files** — all switch from `join(import.meta.dir, "../../fleet")` to `FLEET_DIR`  ### Good - No breaking changes — defaults work same as before - `mkdirSync(FLEET_DIR, { recursive: true

ไม่มีค่า "disable" สำหรับ timer นี้ — มันจะนับเสมอ  แต่ `wait_bluetooth_secs` เป็น `uint32_t` (`config.pb.h:418`) ไม่ใช่ uint16 — ดังนั้นตั้งได้สูงสุด **4294967295** (UINT32_MAX) = ~136 ปี  65535 เป็นแค่ค่าที่เราเลือก (18 ชม.) ตั้งสูงกว่านี้ได้ เช่น **86400** (24 ชม.) หรือ **604800** (7 วัน)  สำหรับ field use 65535 (18 ชม.) น่าจะพอ — เปิดตั้งแต่เช้า BLE ยังอยู่ถึงดึก ต้องการเปลี่ยนเป็นค่าอื่นไหม?

ไม่จำเป็นสำหรับ **build** firmware — node ID ถูก generate จาก MAC address ของ ESP32 ตอน boot ไม่ได้อยู่ใน firmware  แต่จำเป็นสำหรับ **ชื่อ** ที่ bake — เพราะ naming convention ใช้ 4 hex สุดท้ายของ node ID เช่น `FMT4-c4e4`  ถ้าไม่รู้ node ID ก็ bake ชื่อไม่ได้ มี 2 ทางเลือก:  1. **Flash default ก่อน** → `--info` ดู ID → rebuild ด้วยชื่อถูกต้อง 2. **ตั้งชื่อไม่มี hex** เช่น `FMT4` / `T004` แทน `FMT4-XXXX` / `T004` — ไม่ต้องรู้ ID ก่อน build  แบ�

OK. Multi-branch approach:  - `main` — non-compensated (original) - `fix/nc-compensate-temp-humid` — compensated version  The build script fix (server pull + `--clean`) is useful on both branches though. Want me to:  1. Cherry-pick the build script commit (`c91cae86`) to main so both branches have it? 2. Push this branch as-is?

That works too. Keep `main` as non-compensated, and this branch (`fix/nc-compensate-temp-humid`) as the compensated version. When you build:  - `git checkout main` → `./build-nc.sh 639 680` → deploys non-compensated - `git checkout fix/nc-compensate-temp-humid` → `./build-nc.sh 639 680` → deploys compensated  The build scripts now pull from server first, so switching branches won't wipe other devices' builds.  Downside: you need to keep the branch in sync with main for any other changes (display fixes, new features, etc.). But it's the simplest approach — no template refactoring needed.  Want to go with this, or prefer the single-main approach?

Good point. The build scripts build from the device YAML configs, not the template name directly. So if nc639 includes `07_sensor.yml` and nc640 includes `07_sensor_compensated.yml`, the build scripts work as-is — they just compile whatever each config references.  But you'd need to **edit each device config** to switch the include. For 42 devices that's tedious.  Better approach — **use a substitution variable** in the device config:  ```yaml # In each device config (e.g., nc639.yml) substitutions:   sensor_template: "07_sensor_compensated.yml"  # or "07_sensor.yml" ```  ```yaml # In app.yml or wherever the include is - <<: !include { file: ../templates/${sensor_template} } ```  Then to switch a range of devices, just change the substitution in `gen.py` or the device template.  But I
